title: Silly Hypothetical
wikitext:
[]: Silly Hypothetical             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Sat Aug 14, 2010 6:09 am So, I was thinking. If at the end of episode 23, Shinji had accepted Misato's "comfort," would have been even more screwed up that he all ready was or even more so? Also, my personnel view on this subject is that we would have broken out into tears halfway through it. But this may just me thinking that since he is a mentally unstable fourteen year old, he doesn't exactly have the greatest life skills.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Aug 14, 2010 8:56 am It would´ve been the creepiest sex scene ever. OT: It wouldn´t have changed much, IMHO. The fact that Misato EVEN OFFERED sex is already fucked up enough. (That´s exactly why I can´t "use" a lot of Evangelion Doujinshis.)

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Sat Aug 14, 2010 9:01 am Well, for starters, Shinji probably wouldn't have even gotten to the halfway point. And I seriously doubt that she was really willing to have sex with him. a lot of the characters could have used some sort of tension release, but its implied through the sexually active characters that sex is only going to create more pain and frustration.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Aug 14, 2010 9:07 am Holy Diver wrote: And I seriously doubt that she was really willing to have sex with him. a lot of the characters could have used some sort of tension release, but its implied through the sexually active characters that sex is only going to create more pain and frustration.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Sat Aug 14, 2010 9:21 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: It´s implied that Misato´s idea of relaxing and maintaining relationships is simply just sex.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Aug 14, 2010 1:17 pm Holy Diver wrote: Well, for starters, Shinji probably wouldn't have even gotten to the halfway point.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 14, 2010 1:41 pm Tines' scenario would be likely, I'd say. On a personal relationship level between Misato and Shinji (assuming they actually did have sex--no matter how brief or long), I think that Shinji would become even more withdrawn than in-series, and possibly not even Kaworu's emotional warmth to him could snap Shinji out of it. Misato, in contrast, would probably feel even more guilty and horrified than she already was at that point, probably having similar ashamed feelings (or those feelings cranked up to 11 considering they would have done the deed) in TV 25. How exactly Shinji and Misato having had sex affected the rest of the relationships and the story though, I'm not really sure.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 14, 2010 2:33 pm Even if Shinji hadn't pulled the almost completely inexplicable "Don't touch me!" or if Misato had persisted and convinced him, they would have been interrupted by the Nerv hotline going off before anything too interesting occurred. The real question is whether they would pick up where they left off after returning from the hospital. I'm thinking Shinji might be highly curious but unable to really say anything, and Misato, given some time to think about what she almost did, wouldn't bring it up again. Basically things get even more awkward with Shinji feeling used/rejected and Misato in an even deeper shame spiral.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Sat Aug 14, 2010 3:26 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Is that a circumlocution for saying that he'd be an extreme premature ejaculator? I can definitely see him shooting his load before getting down to business, then, being unable to get it up again, fleeing in a sticky mess into the bathroom, locking himself in, and having an Asuka-style self-loathing scene

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Sat Aug 14, 2010 3:30 pm I think this is an interesting topic, hooray~ EDITED FOR MAXIMUM CLARITY Physical intimacy has many forms beyond PENIS VAGINA LOLOLOL. Isn't it Ritsuko who says Misato and Kaji spent a month retreating from the world to fuck? We don't know how much of that was spent having conversations or hanging out. Most of the scenes with Kaji and Misato together (in NGE and Rebuild alike) show them talking, dating, like any other couple. Anyway, I've assumed in the scene in question that Misato's saying she can't do anything about the source of Shinji's problems, but she can "be there" for him. I'm assuming she was going for a cuddle/huggle/whatever, not for hard fucking. Crying on her shoulder might be the emotional catharsis Shinji (an emotionally repressed de facto double-orphan) really needed. He's afraid of people rejecting him. He not afraid of people. Kaworu's earnest sincerity puts Shinji at ease, while everyone else is ambiguous and mysterious. If Misato, Asuka, or Rei were more like Kaworu, I'm sure Shinji would have enjoyed more physical intimacy before the great tanging. There are plenty of scenes where Shinji seems happy to accept Misato's feelings of concern for him, but she isn't consistently earnest. Consider Shinji's kiss with Asuka. If she hadn't acted like it was gross, I imagine they could have made some progress. Consider his kiss with Misato, where her death sort of ruins the mood. Consider his mother, who abandons him by "dying". Consider his father, who, well, you know. Shinji's used to things starting nice and then turning sour. Ultimately, I think Shinji is afraid of starting things with people (be they conversations or kisses) because he thinks partway through they'll reject him. Having that theory proven wrong would help him out, I imagine.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Aug 14, 2010 4:06 pm Shinji would've been all like "Awwiiiight" and his manliness would shoot up to like 11 because really all of Shinji's problems could've been solved by getting laid mirite? Then the world would've been saved and we'd of gotten a typical upbeat shonen mecha anime ending and then Rei, Asuka, and Misato would fight over Shinji harem-style. OH HOW WACKY THAT WOULD BE

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Aug 14, 2010 5:09 pm I propose this thread of massive hypotheticals be moved to chit-chat. I would have been content for her to just give him a hug. It kills me the guy never has any kind of positive physical contact. The hand holding with Misato and the embracing of Rei in rebuild is definitely a marked departure from the series in that respect.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sat Aug 14, 2010 8:42 pm If they had actually had sex I don't think it would have made things any better for either of them. As many have already pointed out I think it would just end up being one more problem for them to deal with.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Aug 14, 2010 10:30 pm i dunno, on some level he has to be aware she cares for him. In episode 23 he's freaked out by whats happening around him moreso than hating himself. The scene vague, so we don't know exactly what her approach really would have been. As I said earlier the whole physical closeness thing would be a new thing for him entirely. I mean, the notion of her being able to seduce him in his vulnerable state isn't too far out there I feel. Long term I think it would be a terrible thing for him, as he's a vulnerable hurt child and she's still grieving for Kaji. Misato aside, I feel like although they care for each other, it would be the kind of thing that would alienate him from her and/or make him feel guilty for doing something immoral or improper.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Aug 14, 2010 11:01 pm Holy Diver wrote: Nailed it!

[]:             Quote Post by SaikiEva » Sun Aug 15, 2010 3:09 am Given how morally inappropriate it would be as Misato is his mother figure, I would say it could even surpass the effects him masturbating over Asuka in EoE had, much earlier in time.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Aug 16, 2010 2:02 pm to be honest i always got the feeling that even if Shinji hadn't rejected the offer, Misato wouldn't have gone through with it. whether because of Kaji's memory, or because she knows it won't solve anything, or because it finally clicks in her brain that HOLY SHIT THIS IS SO WRONG ON SO MANY LEVELS, i think at some point she would've stopped and stepped back. for the sake of the hypothetical scenario, though, if they had actually had sex i imagine it would've been devastating for their already on-the-rocks relationship-- Shinji would likely feel disgusted at the idea of using sex to escape from his problems (i can imagine him having a reaction similar to the one he had in EoE, the infamous "i'm so fucked up" moment) . i think he would also be angry at Misato for putting him in that position. and Misato, well, there's no limit to the self-hatred she would feel after the fact. from the way she felt when she had sex with Kaji (who was not three-quarters down the road to eternal shell-shock, was an active participant, an adult and was actually in love with her, but it still made her feel dirty and tainted) , i can imagine her feeling horrible about it regardless, feeling like she was relapsing to her old ways of coping again, and maybe she'll also have the idea that she "forced him" into it somehow. she hated using sex as an outlet, despite the fact that she could not find any other way to cope-- and it would be horrible for her to think that she had drawn Shinji, who didn't know any better, into it, as well. i have no doubt she would feel like the lowest of the low. corrupting innocence is a powerful thing, and it elicits powerful responses; while some people get off on that, i think Misato would feel horrified about it.

If at the end of episode 23, Shinji had accepted Misato's "comfort," would have been even more screwed up that he all ready was or even more so? Also, my personnel view on this subject is that we would have broken out into tears halfway through it. But this may just me thinking that since he is a mentally unstable fourte
